name: Oracle FLEXCUBE Core Banking API
what_it_does: Oracle FLEXCUBE Universal Banking REST APIs for core banking system platform. Enables AI agents to manage customer account and balance data retrieval for banking operations and customer service, handle loan origination and servicing data management for credit operations, access deposit and treasury operations data for banking analytics, retrieve trade finance and letter of credit data for corporate banking, manage payment processing and fund transfer data for banking operations, handle Islamic banking product data for Sharia-compliant financial services, access regulatory reporting data for Basel, AML, and central bank reporting, retrieve customer KYC and onboarding status data for compliance workflows, manage branch operations and teller transaction data, and integrate core banking data with digital banking, risk, and enterprise banking platforms.
use_cases: Retrieving account balances from customer service banking agents
Processing loan applications from credit operations agents
Accessing payment status from treasury operations agents
Monitoring regulatory compliance from banking compliance agents
not_for: Non-banking financial services without core banking system requirements
Fintech without enterprise banking compliance and regulatory obligations
Credit unions without commercial banking and corporate finance focus
best_when: A commercial bank or financial institution running Oracle FLEXCUBE wants AI agents to automate account servicing, loan operations, payment processing, regulatory reporting, and customer onboarding workflows.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Core banking system automation affects customer financial accounts and regulatory reporting — automated transactions without proper authorization and audit controls violate banking regulations (BSA, AML). Loan decisioning automation requires fair lending compliance review; automated credit decisions must comply with ECOA, FCRA, and fair lending requirements. Core banking access requires strict privileged access management and audit logging.

auth.notes: Oracle FLEXCUBE uses OAuth 2.0 via Oracle API Gateway. Per-institution credentials. FLEXCUBE is on-premise primary with Oracle Cloud options. No webhooks — event integration via Oracle Messaging. REST API with JSON (FLEXCUBE 14.x+). Older versions use SOAP/XML web services. Austin, Texas HQ (Oracle). FLEXCUBE used by 600+ banks in 145+ countries.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise license with Oracle support
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise license; module licensing and user count; implementation and support significant
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas (Oracle Corporation). FLEXCUBE originally developed by i-flex Solutions, acquired by Oracle (2005, $909M). Core banking platform. 600+ banks in 145+ countries. Strong in Asia Pacific, Middle East, and Africa markets. Oracle Banking suite alongside FLEXCUBE. Competes with Temenos and Finastra for core banking market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits via Oracle API Gateway configuration; per-institution setup

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Core banking automation requires dual-control and privileged access management; automated financial transactions without authorization controls violate banking regulations
On-premise deployment — most FLEXCUBE deployments are on-premise; Oracle Cloud FLEXCUBE is available but most banks run on-premise; network access requires VPN or hosted deployment
SOAP to REST migration — FLEXCUBE 14.x introduced REST APIs; older versions use SOAP/XML; verify API version compatibility for your FLEXCUBE deployment version
Oracle API Gateway dependency — FLEXCUBE REST requires Oracle API Gateway configuration; raw FLEXCUBE API access is not directly exposed; gateway setup required
AML/KYC compliance — automated customer data access must follow bank AML/KYC policies and need-to-know access controls; broad data access violates privacy and compliance requirements
Fair lending compliance — any automated loan decisioning must comply with ECOA and fair lending requirements; disparate impact analysis required for automated credit decisions
agent_readiness.security_notes: Core banking system. SOC2, PCI-DSS, AML, Basel3. OAuth2. Global/on-premise. Banking account, loan, and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Core banking. SOC2, PCI-DSS, AML. OAuth2. Global. Customer banking and transaction data.
